audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Galileans/tower warning, fig tree parable, Sabbath healing of bent woman, mustard/leaven, narrow door, and Jerusalem lament.

verses.restored: At that same time some were present reporting to him about the Galileans whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.
He answered them, Do you think these Galileans became greater sinners/misaligned ones than all the Galileans because they suffered these things?
No, I say to you; but unless you turn/realign, you will all likewise perish.
Or those eighteen on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them, do you think they became debtors beyond all the people dwelling in Jerusalem?
No, I say to you; but unless you turn/realign, you will all likewise perish.
He spoke this parable: A certain person had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it and found none.
He said to the vineyard-worker, Look, three years I have come seeking fruit on this fig tree and found none. Cut it down. Why should it make the ground useless?
But he answered, Lord, leave it also this year, until I dig around it and put manure.
If it makes fruit in the future, good; but if not, you will cut it down.
He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
Look, a woman had a spirit of weakness eighteen years; she was bent over and not able to straighten herself completely.
Seeing her, Jesus called out and said to her, Woman, you have been released from your weakness.
He placed hands on her, and immediately she was made straight, and she glorified the Divine.

The synagogue ruler, indignant because Jesus healed on the Sabbath, answered the crowd, There are six days in which it is necessary to work; therefore come and be healed on them, and not on the Sabbath day.
The Lord answered him, Hypocrites, does not each of you on the Sabbath loose his ox or donkey from the manger and lead it away to water?
And this daughter of Abraham, whom the Adversary bound, look, eighteen years, was it not necessary to loose her from this bond on the Sabbath day?
As he said these things, all those opposing him were put to shame, and the whole crowd rejoiced over all the glorious things happening through him.
He said, What is the reign of the Divine like, and to what shall I compare it?
It is like a mustard seed that a person took and threw into his garden; it grew and became a tree, and the birds of heaven nested in its branches.
Again he said, To what shall I compare the reign of the Divine?
It is like leaven that a woman took and hid in three measures of flour until all was leavened.
He traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his journey toward Jerusalem.
Someone said to him, Lord, are few being saved/healed? He said to them,
Strive to enter through the narrow door, because many, I say to you, will seek to enter and will not be strong enough.
Once the house-master rises and shuts the door, and you begin to stand outside and knock, saying, Lord, open to us, he will answer, I do not know where you are from.
Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank before you, and you taught in our streets.
He will say, I do not know where you are from; depart from me, all workers of injustice.
There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in the reign of the Divine, but yourselves cast outside.
They will come from east and west, north and south, and recline in the reign of the Divine.
Look, some are last who will be first, and some are first who will be last.
At that hour some Pharisees came and said to him, Go out and depart from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
He said to them, Go tell that fox, Look, I cast out unclean spirits and complete healings today and tomorrow, and on the third day I am completed.
Nevertheless, it is necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day, because it is not fitting for a prophet to perish outside Jerusalem.
Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the one killing the prophets and stoning those sent to her. How often I wanted to gather your children as a hen gathers her brood under wings, and you were not willing.
Look, your house is left to you. I say to you, you will certainly not see me until you say, Blessed is the one coming in the name of the Lord.
